name: Petróleo Brasileiro (Petrobras)
overview: State-controlled integrated energy major and one of the world's lowest-cost deepwater producers. Its pre-salt assets, dividend policy, and government influence make it a bellwether for Brazilian equities and a key swing barrel for global supply.

exposures.note: Core upstream/downstream operations and pre-salt production base.
Crude export flows into Indian refiners under spot and term deals.

supplyChain: Offshore drilling rigs and FPSO vessels (global shipyard backlog).
Specialized subsea equipment and turbines from international OEMs.
investmentImplications: Dividend trajectory is policy-sensitive; signals from the board precede major share moves.
Pre-salt licensing changes directly affect long-run reserve economics.
A leading proxy for Brazil sovereign risk and the real.

name: Vale S.A.
overview: The world's largest iron-ore producer and a major nickel and copper miner. Vale sits at the intersection of Chinese steel demand and the energy-transition metals complex, making its volume and pricing commentary globally significant.

exposures.note: Iron-ore, nickel and copper operations; logistics rail and ports.
Nickel operations exposed to Indonesian downstreaming policy.

supplyChain: Heavy mining equipment and autonomous haul trucks.
Bulk-carrier shipping capacity to Asian steel mills.
investmentImplications: Iron-ore pricing is hostage to Chinese steel output and stimulus.
Nickel exposure links it directly to Indonesian supply policy and EV demand.
Capital-returns discipline is a key re-rating lever.

name: Nu Holdings (Nubank)
overview: Latin America's largest digital bank, scaling rapidly across Brazil, Mexico and Colombia. A pure-play on the region's financial-inclusion and digitization theme with industry-leading unit economics.

exposures.note: Core deposit, credit-card and lending franchise.
Fast-growing deposit and credit expansion.

investmentImplications: Mexico expansion is the key incremental growth vector to monitor.
Asset-quality trends drive sentiment more than headline user growth.
Rate-cut cycles support consumer-credit demand.

name: Reliance Industries
overview: India's largest company spanning refining and petrochemicals, the Jio telecom and digital platform, and a vast organized-retail network. Its capital-allocation decisions ripple across multiple sectors of the Indian economy.

exposures.note: Refining, telecom (Jio), retail and new-energy build-out.
Crude sourcing relationships for refining feedstock.

supplyChain: Solar-module and battery supply for new-energy gigacomplex.
investmentImplications: Jio tariff hikes are a near-term earnings catalyst widely tracked locally.
New-energy gigacomplex is a multi-year optionality story.
Potential subsidiary listings could unlock conglomerate-discount value.

name: Tata Motors
overview: India's leading commercial-vehicle and EV maker and owner of Jaguar Land Rover. A geared play on Indian auto demand, premiumization, and the domestic electric-vehicle transition.

exposures.note: Passenger, commercial and EV manufacturing; domestic demand.
Battery-cell and component sourcing for EV programs.

supplyChain: EV battery cells and packs (Korean and domestic suppliers).
investmentImplications: Domestic EV market-share defense is critical as competition intensifies.
JLR free-cash-flow and net-debt reduction drive the equity story.
Battery-supply contracts affect EV cost competitiveness.

name: Infosys
overview: A global IT-services and digital-transformation leader. Its deal-pipeline and discretionary-spend commentary is a closely watched read-through for enterprise tech budgets worldwide.

exposures.note: Delivery base and the bulk of its global talent pool.
Nearshore delivery centers serving North American clients.

investmentImplications: Large-deal TCV is the leading indicator for revenue acceleration.
Generative-AI offerings are both an opportunity and a pricing risk.
A bellwether for global enterprise IT-spend sentiment.

name: Grupo Bimbo
overview: The world's largest baking company with deep distribution across the Americas. A defensive consumer-staples name leveraged to input-cost cycles, FX, and North American consumption.

exposures.note: Domestic manufacturing and distribution backbone.
Latin American operations and brand portfolio.

supplyChain: Wheat, sugar and edible-oils commodity inputs.
investmentImplications: Wheat and energy input costs are the primary margin swing factor.
A peso-FX play given its US dollar revenue mix.
Defensive characteristics in risk-off regional rotations.

name: Grupo México
overview: One of the world's largest copper producers, with significant transport and infrastructure arms. A primary lever on the global copper supply story central to electrification.

exposures.note: Copper mining, rail transport and infrastructure assets.
Regional logistics and metals trade flows.

supplyChain: Power and water for processing operations.
investmentImplications: A leveraged play on the structural copper-supply deficit.
Permitting and community/water issues are key project risks.
Transport arm provides diversified, defensive cash flow.

name: Bank Central Asia (BCA)
overview: Indonesia's most valuable bank, prized for a low-cost deposit franchise and digital-transaction leadership. A core proxy for Indonesian consumption and the country's financial-deepening story.

exposures.note: Dominant transaction-banking and consumer-lending franchise.

investmentImplications: Best-in-class funding cost supports through-cycle profitability.
Loan growth tracks domestic consumption and capex confidence.
Rupiah stability is central to foreign-investor positioning.

name: Vingroup
overview: Vietnam's largest private conglomerate spanning real estate (Vinhomes), electric vehicles (VinFast) and retail. A high-beta play on Vietnamese growth, property liquidity, and the country's EV ambitions.

exposures.note: Property, EV manufacturing (VinFast) and retail operations.

supplyChain: EV battery cells and powertrain components.
investmentImplications: Property-segment liquidity is the key solvency and sentiment driver.
VinFast funding needs weigh on group balance-sheet perception.
Highly geared to Vietnamese policy support and rate direction.

name: Samsung Electronics
overview: The world's largest memory-chip maker and a leading smartphone and display manufacturer. Its memory-pricing and HBM-qualification commentary is a primary leading indicator for the global AI-hardware cycle.

exposures.note: Core memory, foundry and display manufacturing base.
Large smartphone and electronics assembly operations.
Growing manufacturing footprint and domestic demand.

supplyChain: Assembly and test operations across Asia.
investmentImplications: HBM qualification with AI-accelerator customers is the key catalyst.
Memory-pricing inflection drives the bulk of earnings leverage.
Foundry-share dynamics versus rivals shape the long-term story.

name: SK Hynix
overview: A leading memory-chip maker and the front-runner in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI accelerators. Arguably the purest listed proxy for AI-driven memory demand worldwide.

exposures.note: Primary DRAM/NAND and HBM manufacturing base.
Back-end packaging and test operations.

supplyChain: Advanced packaging and TSV equipment for HBM.
investmentImplications: HBM allocation and pricing power are the central earnings drivers.
Capacity-expansion timing affects the next cycle's supply balance.
A high-beta expression of the AI-infrastructure build-out.

name: Koç Holding
overview: Turkey's largest industrial conglomerate with interests in energy (Tüpraş), automotive, durables and finance. A diversified proxy for Turkish macro normalization and the export-led industrial cycle.

exposures.note: Refining, automotive, white-goods and banking interests.
Export and component-sourcing relationships.

investmentImplications: A geared expression of Turkey's macro-stabilization trade.
Refining margins (Tüpraş) drive a large share of group earnings.
Export-oriented units benefit from a competitive lira.
